title: Derris peckoltii
taxonomy: Derris peckoltii is classified as a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae), native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a climber that belongs to the genus Derris, which consists of approximately 20 species of tropical legumes. The plant's classification is further detailed in the binomial nomenclature system, where it is categorized as a member of the family Fabaceae, subfamily Faboideae, tribe Dalbergieae. Derris peckoltii's classification is essential for understanding its ecological role, growth habits, and potential uses. By understanding its classification, researchers can also identify potential areas for conservation and sustainability initiatives.
uses: Derris peckoltii is a plant species that has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Brazil and other parts of South America. Its roots and leaves are believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. Additionally, Derris peckoltii has been used in traditional medicine to manage digestive issues and as a treatment for certain types of wounds. However, the effectiveness of Derris peckoltii as a medicinal agent has not been extensively scientifically studied, and more research is needed to confirm its potential benefits.

constituents: Derris peckoltii contains active constituents such as rotenone and desmethylrotenone, which are responsible for its insecticidal properties. These compounds are toxic to aquatic animals and have been used as a piscicide. Additionally, Derris peckoltii also contains other compounds like desacetylderrisin and isoderrisin, which contribute to its overall toxicity. The active constituents in Derris peckoltii have been used in various applications, including insect control and fish poisoning. However, the use of Derris peckoltii has been restricted due to its potential environmental impact and toxicity.

side_effects: I couldn't find any information on the specific side effects of Derris peckoltii, but Derris peckoltii is a plant that contains rotenone, which is toxic to humans and animals. intro: Derris peckoltii, also known as Peckoltia, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include antiparasitic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating various conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Derris peckoltii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Derris peckoltii is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and also used to make herbal teas and tinctures.
